summary: Solid mid‑summer 1992 set with standout Deal and strong second half
blurb: The show opens with a bright Touch of Grey and builds to an electrifying Deal where Jerry’s run is otherworldly. The second set delivers The Wheel into Black Peter and a joyous Good Lovin', showcasing the band’s tight groove. Overall it’s a solid performance that shines especially on the soundboard recording.
review: On June 17, 1992 at Charlotte Coliseum the Dead delivered a reliable summer‑tour outing that balances the occasional wobble of the first set with a scorching second half. The opening stretch features Touch of Grey and Greatest Story Ever Told before slipping into a gritty Big River and an unforgettable Deal—Jerry’s solo described by fans as “otherworldly” and one of his best. After a brief intermission the band fires up with China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider, then rolls into a tight Estimated Prophet that segues into a crisp Drum‑Space‑The Wheel sequence, followed by Black Peter and a jubilant Good Lovin'. The encore U.S. Blues caps the night on a high note. While audience recordings capture the energy with some muffled fidelity, the Miller soundboard tape offers crystal‑clear detail, making it the preferred listen for anyone wanting to hear this solid 1992 performance in its best light.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: One recording is explicitly positive, praising the show as consistently strong; the other is mixed but still highlights several standout songs (Deal, Good Lovin', The Wheel, etc.) and only notes minor set‑list unevenness. The overall consensus leans positive across multiple sources, justifying a moderate +1.0 sentiment boost.

best_recording.reason: Excellent soundboard capture with crystal‑clear, well‑balanced audio; provides the most detailed listening experience of this show.

key_highlights: Jerry’s otherworldly solo on Deal stands out as a career highlight.
The second set’s The Wheel > Black Peter > Good Lovin' sequence showcases tight group chemistry.
Soundboard recording delivers pristine clarity, making the performance easy to appreciate.
Overall solid 1992 summer‑tour show with a strong blend of rockers and jams.

band_performance.Jerry: Delivers an otherworldly run on Deal and maintains strong, melodic lead throughout the set.
